name: Sleuth DORA Metrics API
what_it_does: DORA metrics and deployment tracking platform with REST API — enabling engineering teams to track deployments as 'changes', automatically calculate DORA metrics (deployment frequency, lead time for changes, change failure rate, MTTR), correlate deployments with incidents, and integrate with CI/CD pipelines, GitHub, LaunchDarkly, and PagerDuty for a complete change intelligence view.
use_cases: Tracking deployments as changes via Sleuth API for DORA metrics automation
Registering deployment events via Sleuth API for deployment frequency measurement
Correlating changes with incidents via Sleuth API for change failure rate calculation
Measuring lead time for changes via Sleuth API from commit to production
Integrating CI/CD pipeline data via Sleuth API for end-to-end deployment analytics
not_for: Broad engineering analytics beyond deployment tracking (use LinearB/DX for broader metrics)
Real-time infrastructure monitoring (use Datadog/New Relic for observability)
best_when: An engineering team wants deployment-centric DORA metrics with minimal setup — Sleuth's 'change' model wraps deployments, feature flags, and config changes into a unified view for elite DORA measurement.
avoid_when: You need broader engineering analytics (Git trends, PR cycle time) beyond deployment tracking, or want a comprehensive developer experience platform.

auth.notes: API key via apikey query parameter or Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Sleuth organization settings. REST API at app.sleuth.io/api/v1/. Webhook support for deployment events. GitHub/GitLab integration for commit-to-deploy lead time. PagerDuty/OpsGenie for incident correlation. LaunchDarkly for feature flag change tracking. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Jenkins integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 3 projects; limited metrics history
pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco). Founded 2019 by Don Brown and Priyanka Nag. Acquired by Jellyfish (engineering management platform) in 2023. Sleuth 'change' model: deployments + feature flags + config changes tracked as 'changes' to a 'deployment'. DORA metrics automated from change data. Environments: dev/staging/production. Impact tracking: correlate changes with error rates (Sentry, Datadog), incidents (PagerDuty). Elite performer benchmarking: 4 DORA tiers. LaunchDarkly integration: feature flag rollouts counted as changes. Post-acquisition by Jellyfish: product direction may evolve.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API key in query param OR Bearer header. Change model (not just deployments). Acquired by Jellyfish 2023. Free tier available. Webhooks. GitHub/PagerDuty/LaunchDarkly integrations. Monthly contracts.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests idempotent. POST to register changes: use sha (commit SHA) for idempotent change registration — same SHA = same change. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: API key can be passed as query param (?apikey=KEY) or Authorization: Bearer — query param is less secure
Sleuth 'change' model: register deployments as changes (not just deployments) — includes feature flags, config changes
SHA (commit SHA) used as idempotent change identifier — same SHA won't create duplicate changes
Acquired by Jellyfish (2023) — product/API direction may change; verify current roadmap
Environment-specific changes: org slug and deployment slug required in API paths
LaunchDarkly integration counts feature flag rollouts as 'changes' — DORA metrics include flags

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key: prefer Authorization: Bearer over query param (avoid logging exposure). SOC2 certified. Deployment data is sensitive operational IP. GitHub/GitLab OAuth integration credentials must be secured. PagerDuty integration for incident correlation. Post-Jellyfish acquisition: verify security posture continuity.
